avoid: Replacing cuBLAS for the full W @ X GEMM with a custom kernel; high risk and unlikely to beat cuBLAS.
Hardcoding hidden dimensions or tensor sizes; the operator must remain shape-agnostic within the given range.
evidence: Speedup is ~1.0 across all shapes, indicating no performance improvement over the PyTorch baseline.
Latency is identical to torch baseline within measurement noise.
Profile summary confirms bottleneck is multiple ATen kernel launches for W@X, B.T@X, A@(B.T@X), and the final add.
Correctness passes with zero error, so no correctness issues.
recommended_actions: Keep cuBLAS for the large W @ X GEMM; it is already optimal.
Fuse the rank-16 update (A @ (B.T @ X)) and the addition into a single custom kernel to reduce launch overhead and improve memory locality.
Explore vectorized loads/stores and unrolling in the fused rank-16 kernel to maximize bandwidth utilization.
Consider shape-aware dispatch if a single fused kernel does not perform uniformly across d in [3584, 4608].
